architecture.summary: Masked fine-tuning for autoregressive LLMs (arLLMs) reframes knowledge documents as token-reconstruction tasks. During fine-tuning, a fraction of tokens in a document are replaced with [MASK]; the model is prompted to recover the full passage. The training objective (Eq. 2) computes autoregressive loss only on unmasked tokens in the assistant response. Mask ratios are sampled uniformly across epochs, providing varied conditioning patterns. At inference, the model is evaluated in standard autoregressive mode using default chat templates. The mechanism transfers the bidirectional learning signal of diffusion LLMs (dLLMs) into decoder-only arLLMs without architectural changes.
trained_components: dLLM parameters (during fine-tuning with demasking objective)

biblio.title: Diffusion-Inspired Masked Fine-Tuning for Knowledge Injection in Autoregressive LLMs

claims.statement: On NameDescription dataset, arLLM without paraphrases achieves 0.264 forward accuracy and 0.029 backward accuracy; with paraphrases, forward reaches 0.954 but backward remains 0.031.
dLLM (LLaDA-8B-Instruct) without paraphrases achieves 0.869 forward and 0.852 backward accuracy on NameDescription; with paraphrases, 0.980 forward and 0.984 backward.
Masked fine-tuning for arLLM on NameDescription achieves high accuracy in both forward and backward directions without paraphrases, closing the gap to dLLM performance.
On Webscale-RL (1.2M samples), masked-AR SFT achieves 0.389 GPQA-diamond accuracy, outperforming standard SFT AR at 0.359 and base model at 0.242.
On GSM8K, Llama-3.2-3B masked SFT achieves 0.735 pass@1, improving over baseline 0.686. On MATH, Llama-3.2-3B masked SFT achieves 0.290 pass@1 versus baseline 0.258.
Masked fine-tuning for arLLMs does not require paraphrase augmentation to generalize knowledge to question-answering, in contrast to standard autoregressive fine-tuning.
dLLMs are substantially less reliant on paraphrase augmentation and exhibit markedly reduced reversal-curse behavior compared to arLLMs during post-training knowledge injection.

claims.metric: training compute (FLOP ratio and convergence speed)
claims.statement: Masked fine-tuning doubles per-step FLOPs but converges 2× faster, yielding comparable or faster total training compute than standard arLLM and dLLM fine-tuning.
